tasks.final_answer:
Pinot Noir, Merlot, and Cabernet Sauvignon are all great choices for pairing with steak. As a general rule, leaner steaks are best with light or medium-bodied red wines like Pinot Noir or Merlot. Fattier steaks pair well with a bolder red, like a Cabernet Sauvignon.

For example, St. Innocent Freedom Hill Pinot Noir is a good option. It has an average rating of 4.8 out of 5 stars and costs about $34 per bottle.
tasks.judge_reasoning: The agent correctly used the get_wine_pairing tool and provided the recommended wine types along with a brief description as requested.

result.pairingText: Steak works really well with Pinot Noir, Merlot, and Cabernet Sauvignon. After all, beef and red wine are a classic combination. Generally, leaner steaks go well with light or medium-bodied reds, such as pinot noir or merlot, while fattier steaks can handle a bold red, such as cabernet sauvingnon. You could try St. Innocent Freedom Hill Pinot Noir. Reviewers quite like it with a 4.8 out of 5 star rating and a price of about 34 dollars per bottle.

productMatches.title: St. Innocent Freedom Hill Pinot Noir
productMatches.description: The nose reveals an enchanting mixture of black cherries, pepper, cassis, and dark berries. Dark fruit and dried flowers dominate the mouth with a clay-like earthiness adding texture to the viscosity that fills your mouth. Expect steady evolution of the fruit and softening of the tannins with up to 12 years of bottle age. Perfect for that Porterhouse or T-bone. Open at least one hour before drinking.

result.instructions:
In a small bowl mix all the spices including the ginger. Set aside.
In a large saucepan, heat the oil .
Add the onions and cook until golden brown.
Add the garlic and continue to cook for a minute.
Stir in the spices and allow the flavours to infuse.
Stir in the tomato paste.
Add the chopped tomato and pour in the water. Simmer and season with salt to taste.
Add the chicken pieces stirring well to coat the meat with the sauce. Continue to simmer until the chicken is cooked and the sauce has thickened, about 12 minutes.
Stir in the yogurt and continue to simmer 5 minutes.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro
Serve with bismati rice.

steps.step: In a small bowl mix all the spices including the ginger. Set aside.
In a large saucepan, heat the oil .
Add the onions and cook until golden brown.
Add the garlic and continue to cook for a minute.
Stir in the spices and allow the flavours to infuse.
Stir in the tomato paste.
Add the chopped tomato and pour in the water. Simmer and season with salt to taste.
Add the chicken pieces stirring well to coat the meat with the sauce. Continue to simmer until the chicken is cooked and the sauce has thickened, about 12 minutes.
Stir in the yogurt and continue to simmer 5 minutes.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro
Serve with bismati rice.

result.instructions:
Season the salmon fillets with some salt, pepper and a pinch of paprika and keep aside.
Preheat the oven to 200 C.  Roughly dice the potatoes, parsnips and carrots and add to a roasting tray. Drizzle over the olive oil and season with salt and pepper. Mix well and roast for 15 minutes. Add in the onion and roast for a further 10-15 minutes
Place the salmon fillets and tomatoes between the veg. Drizzle the lemon juice and sprinkle over the rosemary and thyme. Season lightly with salt and pepper and roast for 10-15 minutes or until the salmon and veg is cooked through.
Serve with some green salad.

steps.step: Season the salmon fillets with some salt, pepper and a pinch of paprika and keep aside.
Preheat the oven to 200 C.  Roughly dice the potatoes, parsnips and carrots and add to a roasting tray.
Drizzle over the olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Mix well and roast for 15 minutes.
Add in the onion and roast for a further 10-15 minutes
Place the salmon fillets and tomatoes between the veg.
Drizzle the lemon juice and sprinkle over the rosemary and thyme. Season lightly with salt and pepper and roast for 10-15 minutes or until the salmon and veg is cooked through.
Serve with some green salad.

result.instructions:
1. Pre-Heat oven to 350 F
  2. Season each salmon filet with sea salt and pepper
  3. Place on a greased sheet tray
  4. Combine olive oil, lemon juice, Panko and chopped dill in a bowl
  5. Fold Feta Crumbles into breadcrumb mixture
  6. Carefully coat the top of each salmon filet with the breadcrumb mixture
  7. Bake in the oven for 8-10 minutes until topping becomes golden brown
  8. Serve over couscous with a sprig of dill and fresh lemon

steps.step: Pre-
Heat oven to 350 FSeason each salmon filet with sea salt and pepper
Place on a greased sheet tray
Combine olive oil, lemon juice, Panko and chopped dill in a bowl
Fold Feta Crumbles into breadcrumb mixture
Carefully coat the top of each salmon filet with the breadcrumb mixture
Bake in the oven for 8-10 minutes until topping becomes golden brown
Serve over couscous with a sprig of dill and fresh lemon
